title: Preparing for Junior High: What You Need to Know?
excerpt: The move to junior high is a jump into algebra, and without a few foundation topics it is hard. The list of topics to close before the end of sixth grade, why exactly these, and how to prepare.
author: Smart Worksheets Team
content:
Junior High Is a Jump Into Algebra

The move to seventh grade is not just another year, it is a change in the nature of math itself. In elementary school we worked mostly with numbers. In junior high, you start working with letters, equations, and rules. That jump is hard for kids who arrive with a shaky base, and easy for those who closed the foundation topics in time. So here is what really matters to close before the end of sixth grade.

The Topics You Must Close

  • Fractions. Adding, subtracting, and reducing, with confidence. Algebra is full of fractions, and a child who struggles with them will be worn down.
  • Percentages. Finding a percent of a number and understanding an after-discount price. This comes back again and again in junior high.
  • Order of operations. What comes first, multiplication or addition, and what to do with the parentheses. Without this, every algebraic expression gets solved wrong.
  • Simple equations. Finding the unknown in a problem like unknown plus 5 equals 12. This is the door to algebra.
  • Negative numbers. The first acquaintance with below zero, which shows up right away in seventh grade.

Why Exactly These

Because each one is a brick that algebra is built on. Take a simple equation like x plus 5 equals 12. To solve it, you need to understand the inverse operation, 12 minus 5 equals 7. That is exactly elementary-school math, just in new clothing. A child who masters inverse operations and the order of operations walks into algebra with almost no effort.

How to Prepare Without Pressure

You do not need to teach algebra in advance. You just need to strengthen the foundations. The summer before seventh grade is a great time, a quarter of an hour a day on the weak topic, no more. Find where the child is shakiest, fractions or order of operations, and focus there. A solid base is worth more than a preview of new material.

It Is Not Only Math, It Is Also Habits

Junior high brings a change that has nothing to do with the material, suddenly there are several teachers, more homework, and more responsibility on the child themselves. A child who arrives from elementary school without organized study habits will struggle, even if they are strong in math. So alongside strengthening the topics, it is worth setting a routine, a fixed place to study, a homework planner, and a habit of checking their own work. Math and organization go hand in hand in seventh grade, and both are built in advance.

Questions Parents Ask

Should we teach algebra before junior high? Not at all. It is better to close the foundations from elementary school. Algebra will come in class, and it will be easy if the base is there.

My child is weak in several topics, where do we start? With fractions and order of operations, they are the most critical for algebra. After that, percentages and simple equations.

What do we practice with? You can strengthen fractions in the fractions generator and percentages in the percentage generator. A quarter of an hour a day on the weak point, regularly, beats one marathon before the school year starts.
